column2: German line says 'Tendenz' (tendency) instead of 'choice'; Japanese and Chinese also say 'tendency'
English says 'rank', but German, Japanese, and Chinese say 'measure of importance'
English says 'likely', but German, Japanese, and Chinese say 'foreseeable'
Definition mismatch: English 'even with that' vs others 'without regard'
German line says 'leises Bewegen' (quiet moving) instead of 'secret move'
German line says 'heimlicher Mensch' (secret person) instead of 'sneaky move'; Japanese and Chinese also refer to a person, not a move
Chinese line uses verb phrase (使惊吓) instead of noun/gerund as in English
English defines 'twice' as 'double', but translations define it as an adverb meaning 'two times' – different core concept.
Chinese line says '步骤' (step) but English says 'procedure' – different category.
